In this 2017 GDC session, Lunarch Studios'  David Churchill describes the Hierarchical Portfolio Search AI system created for Prismata, and how it can be adapted to fit almost any genre of game, including search-heavy ones like strategy games.

Register for GDC: https://ubm.io...
[2020-04-20 16:38] Deleted User (Deleted User): >  If an AI agent can be made that is able to
> play at the level of expert players, the process of game balance and testing could then be automated instead of relying
> solely on human players for feedback. For example, if a designer wants to test a new unit design before releasing it to
> the public they could run millions of AI vs. AI games in
> an attempt to see if the unit is purchased with the desired frequency or if it leads to an imbalance in win percentage for the
> first or second player
holy shit this is revolutionary why haven't i seen other games do/talk about this

[2020-04-20 16:43] elyot: there is NO deckbuilding AI for those games

[2020-04-20 17:17] velizar_ (Name): I don't think Starcraft can benefit from that, either with a theoretical extrapolation of its game AI to make it as good as top players, or with Google's AI, DeepMind.
[2020-04-20 17:17] velizar_ (Name): Its game AI has no APM limits (but currently is much worse than most players)
[2020-04-20 17:18] velizar_ (Name): DeepMind... has a lot of hacks to make it more human-like, that capture the obvious things like EPM (effective actions per minute), but not the nonobvious ones like click accuracy.
[2020-04-20 17:18] velizar_ (Name): It plays the game differently, makes different mistakes ... etc
[2020-04-20 17:19] velizar_ (Name): I've played chess vs AI and it alternated between being mega good (far beyond me at least) and putting its queen in front of my pawn for no reason.

[2020-04-20 22:39] flakmaniak: When you say "regularly beat Master Bot", what do you mean, Elyot? As in, beat it a significant percentage of the time? Or rarely drop a game to it?
[2020-04-20 22:39] flakmaniak: Also, how much does Master Bot's quality vary based on the computer it's being run on?
[2020-04-20 22:40] elyot: even getting 50% winrate against master bot takes most players many many hours of playing, could be 10-30 hours
[2020-04-20 22:41] elyot: and not really, it needs exponentially more time to go even 1 ply deeper in search, which improves its performance only marginally
[2020-04-20 22:41] flakmaniak: Hmm. I guess I don't really think of 10-30 hours as that long a time to learn a game.
[2020-04-20 22:42] flakmaniak: If I've only played a competitive game 10h, I think I've barely even started.
[2020-04-20 22:42] elyot: I know some players that spent several weeks trying to get good enough to beat master bot

[2020-04-30 12:29] flakmaniak: Skywing Apollo is intentionally Gundam Double X, right?
[2020-04-30 12:31] elyot: I know nothing about Gundam but that whole set was drawn by an artist who was a fan of that stuff I think
[2020-04-30 12:32] flakmaniak: The shape of its wings, I guess you'd call them, is just... Well it's Gundam X's reflectors, but six of them like Double X.
[2020-04-30 12:34] elyot: For some of the custom unit sets, I wrote detailed descriptions of them, like bloodrager with a chef's hat splashing frosting all over the place, etc.
[2020-04-30 12:34] elyot: for supermecha, I literally just said "make these dudes look like epic mechas from anime"
[2020-04-30 12:34] elyot: and gave a list of units
[2020-04-30 12:34] elyot: and very little other guidance
[2020-04-30 12:35] elyot: Regarding LOR and randomness, it's just the same thing all the hearthstone devs said. The only thing I disagree with is that RNG is necessary to achieve those goals. Prismata is sorta a case-in-point that you can achieve the same goals via a deterministic game if you design it well. But RNG makes a lot of other game design easier

[2020-06-12 20:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): !! apparently in a game called Zenonzard there's this thing called asynch pvp where the AI builds "profiles" of players and uses machine learning to build decks and come up with moves etc and on ladder you don't play against someone but instead on an AI who plays like the person
[2020-06-12 20:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): if u could do this in pris i think it would change everything
[2020-06-12 20:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): and it helps new players as well since the AI gives tips on what moves to make
[2020-06-12 20:42] anesotericman (Esoteric): The ai emulation of players would be worse than masterbot (without additional AI work), so it wouldn't be useful for masters players to play against
[2020-06-12 20:42] anesotericman (Esoteric): and players below that wouldn't really notice the nuances of player personalities compared to the existing AI profiles in casual.
[2020-06-12 20:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): hmmm i do wonder if then zeonztard would be shitty then
[2020-06-12 20:45] anesotericman (Esoteric): probably would work a lot better if the strategy is less about thinking ahead multiple moves and more about evaluation of boardstates
[2020-06-12 20:45] elyot: I kinda think you'd have to solve a problem that's almost as hard as just making a machine learning AI for Prismata
[2020-06-12 20:46] elyot: it might help to emulate humans for deckbuilding, but for actual gameplay, it's really hard
[2020-06-12 20:46] elyot: Prismata is a game that humans are so good at, they can really identify when the AI is not playing well (unlike in hidden information card games where AI misplays are way more concealed)
[2020-06-12 20:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): well I guess another question is what abt using machine learning to build AI for pris
[2020-06-12 20:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): I think zenon uses machine learning for gameplay for decks which players had built beforehand
[2020-06-12 20:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): but likely the gameplay is simplistic enough
[2020-06-12 20:48] anesotericman (Esoteric): I'm sure you could. My brother's knowledgeable in the field, and doesn't think there's many unique problems to it. It's just a matter of would developer and simulation costs to make a better AI be worth the cost. Probably not?
[2020-06-12 20:48] elyot: we tried doing some learning (was a side project of an intern who was doing a CS degree)
[2020-06-12 20:49] elyot: it worked in the case where the list of units was known
[2020-06-12 20:49] elyot: didn't get good results for random unit sets, too hard to generalize, needs a lot of work and probably a million bucks in electricity to train the model 😛
[2020-06-12 20:51] anesotericman (Esoteric): If you put up a $10k bounty for a 2000-rated bot, my brother would probably take you up on the challenge. He's worked on some machine learning Go AI stuff, so not a complete rando. We assumed the cost-benefit wasn't there for you.
[2020-06-12 20:53] elyot: the problem is that people can just cram it full of heuristics, when what we really want is a bot that would work with any unit that we could ever make in the future
[2020-06-12 20:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): so just do it for the grandmaster set or smth
[2020-06-12 20:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): it might be useful to have a pausable afkable but strong opponent
[2020-06-12 20:58] Deleted User (Deleted User): i do like a scalable generalised bot tho
[2020-06-12 21:00] anesotericman (Esoteric): Prismata's tough in that a lot of the strategic decisions relate to supplies running out in 10 turns (at higher levels), or not having anything good to spend on red in 6 turns. Takes a decent amount of cycles to run through the possibilities but they don't need to be done live, necessarily? Could "train" bots to play in specific sets as needed. This is all assuming you don't add completely new mechanics (like freezing) but just have new variations within current (or otherwise set) limitations.
[2020-06-12 21:01] anesotericman (Esoteric): There's an interesting AI competition here where you say you'll add 40 new units in various combinations for the final challenge and AI would have an hour of training time for self-play/training on each set before the actual game starts or something.
[2020-06-12 21:02] anesotericman (Esoteric): but this is all just on the "cost" side of the cost/benefit analysis. Still not sure that there's much to gain from having a better bot.

@SilentSlayers It never auto-concedes in turns after 1 unless there is a disconnect. If the player is connected and uses all their time every turn, it allows it by default. Current plan is probably to release a premium "complete edition" and just discount it a lot during Steam sales etc.. I don't know when/if we will commission more skins. [2020-07-23 22:44] sano712 (sano): obviously they're talking on average
[2020-07-23 22:46] elyot: We will not be changing ranked to have fewer time controls, if players want to match faster then they can just queue with more options checked
[2020-07-23 22:46] elyot: For B+5/B+8/etc. I do think that more units = more opportunities to make slightly better plays, which would have an effect on k-factor but not rating
[2020-07-23 22:49] elyot: We don't adjust for it though (in an ideal world you'd probably lose/gain fewer rating points for B+5 if we wanted the rating system to be optimal in how it reflects player skill)
[2020-07-23 22:50] elyot: But the practical truth is that players actually prefer rating systems where the points gained/lost reflect some notion of how much effort they feel they put into playing, which is why short games are worth fewer points
[2020-07-23 22:52] elyot: An interesting research question is "how do you estimate what the difference in k-factor is" and it would actually require going through a lot of data

The point is that games where cluster all-in is just the correct strategy intuitively should be less likely when there are more units. Strategies don't exist in a vacuum.
